response.justification: James Smith is a skilled polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and software craftsmanship credentials, but he is a fundamental mismatch for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor's thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge, and his listed skills do not include Python, SQL Server, Oracle, or any relevant messaging frameworks. The only partial alignment is a brief fintech stint at RIDE Capital, which used Node.js/TypeScript, not C++.
strengths: Proven software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) transferable to any engineering context
Broad polyglot background including Java, DLang, and early C/C++ work, showing adaptability across paradigms
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via public talks, workshops, and open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, 2014–2015) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows
Zero exposure to fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or any investment-banking domain
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary data store experience is MongoDB and MySQL in web/SaaS contexts
